what_works: Always use cursor-based pagination for lists. Never offset-based. Cursor: use the last item's created_at + id as the cursor. This avoids the 'page drift' problem when items are added while paginating.

For mutations: 1) save previousData with queryClient.getQueryData, 2) optimistically update with queryClient.setQueryData, 3) onError: restore previousData and show toast, 4) onSettled: invalidate to get server truth. Never skip the rollback.
Use the fromTable<T>('tableName') helper from src/lib/supabase.ts for all database queries. It returns typed data without 'as any' casts. Never call supabase.from() directly without type generics.
Wrap each route in an ErrorBoundary component that catches render errors and shows a user-friendly fallback UI. One ErrorBoundary per route, not per component.
All RLS policies MUST wrap auth.uid() in a subselect: USING (user_id = (select auth.uid())). This prevents per-row re-evaluation and gives a 1,571x performance improvement on large tables.

Computed insights must query for specific entity identifiers (RFI subjects, budget line descriptions, trade names, phase names) and include them in the insight text. 'RFI 047 is 3 days past due' not '1 RFI overdue'. Fallback chain: edge function → computed with entities → computed with counts → static.
what_fails: Batch refactors across 10+ files in one commit. The immune system (homeostasis) rejects these at Tier 2 because too many concurrent changes can't be verified individually.
Adding TODO stubs or empty functions. The quality auditor scores incomplete code WORSE than no code. Never ship stubs.
Using npm ci with Mac-generated lockfiles on Linux CI. Vite 8's rolldown requires platform-specific native bindings. The lockfile from Mac doesn't include Linux bindings.
